articles.headline: Janelle Salaun scores 21 points off the bench and Valkyries beat Mercury 95-79 to win home opener
articles.description: — Janelle Salaun scored 21 points off the bench, Gabby Williams contributed 19 points and five rebounds in her home debut for Golden State after the All-Star forward signed last month, and the Valkyries beat the Phoenix Mercury 95-79 on Sunday night.

articles.headline: Aces rebound from season-opening drubbing with win vs. Sparks
articles.description: In a span of 24 hours, the Aces received their championship rings, suffered a historically bad season-opening loss and then picked up their first win over the Sparks on Sunday.

articles.headline: Carter scores 22, Young adds 20 as Aces beat Sparks 105-78
articles.description: — Chennedy Carter scored 22 points, Jackie Young had 20 points and nine assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat Los Angeles 105-78 on Sunday in the Sparks' season opener.

articles.headline: Johannes scores 25, Stewart adds 23 and 9 rebounds as Liberty beat Mystics 98-93 in OT
articles.description: — Marine Johannes hit six 3-pointers and scored 25 points, Breanna Stewart had 23 points and nine rebounds, and the New York Liberty beat the Washington Mystics 98-93 in overtime on Sunday.

articles.headline: Lexie Brown hits 5 3-pointers and scores 17 as the Storm beat the Sun 89-82
articles.description: — Lexie Brown hit five 3-pointers and scored 17 points, Flau’jae Johnson had 16 and the Seattle Storm beat the Connecticut Sun 89-82 on Sunday.

articles.headline: Caitlin Clark joins Morgan Wallen for Indianapolis concert walkout
articles.description: With Wallen at Lucas Oil Stadium for his "Still the Problem" tour, he tapped into the local hoops scene and was accompanied by Caitlin Clark for his walkout.

article.headline: Iriafen scores a career-high 23 points and the Washington Mystics rally to beat the Dallas Wings
article.description: — Kiki Iriafen scored a career-high 23 points and grabbed 10 rebounds and the Washington Mystics used a strong second half to beat the Dallas Wings 91-78 on Sunday.
article.linkText: Iriafen scores a career-high 23 points and the Washington Mystics rally to beat the Dallas Wings

images.name: Mystics roll past Wings
images.caption: Kiki Iriafen leads all scorers with 23 points as the Mystics defeat the Wings 91-78.

article.story: ARLINGTON, Texas -- — Kiki Iriafen scored a career-high 23 points and grabbed 10 rebounds and the Washington Mystics used a strong second half to beat the Dallas Wings 91-78 on Sunday. Sonia Citron scored 17 of her 18 points in the second half, Stefanie Dolson scored 14 and reserve Emily Engstler had 11 for the Mystics (14-17) who ended a four-game losing streak. Paige Bueckers scored 17 points to lead Dallas and move into a tie for fourth place in WNBA history with Ruthie Bolton with her 25th game scoring in double digits to start a career. Cynthia Cooper is the leader at 92 games, followed by A’ja Wilson with 37 and Candace Parker with 32. Arike Ogunbowale scored 12 points and Haley Jones 11 for Dallas, which has lost five straight. After scoring 30 points in the first half and allowing Dallas (8-24) to build a 41-30 lead, Washington outscored the Wings 33-17 in the third and 28-20 in the fourth. Up 63-58 at the end of the third, Citron buried a 27-foot, step back 3-pointer to make it 76-66 with 5:34 remaining. Dallas led 23-18 at the end of the first quarter. Iriafen's tip-in with 4:57 before halftime brought the Mystics within 28-27, but Washington failed to convert a field before the break and Dallas pushed its lead to 41-30 at intermission with a 13-0 run. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
